q: In a circular motion, the object just moves in a ___.
Rotation of earth about its own axis creates the cycle of ___ and night.
A ___ body is a body that does not deform or change shape.
The distance between any two ___ within a rigid body remains constant.
If the angular velocity of an object changes at a constant rate, the ___ is constant.
The motion is called the rotational motion under ___ angular acceleration if the rate of change of angular velocity is constant.
Rotational variables include angular displacement, angular velocity, and ___.
The kinematic equations for rotational motion are ___ to equations for constant acceleration in linear motion.
The average angular speed is expressed by the equation $\bar{\omega} = \frac{\omega + ___}{2}$.
The equation $\omega = \omega_0 + \alpha t$ relates angular velocity, initial angular velocity, angular acceleration, and ___.
The symbol for angular displacement is ___.
Angular acceleration is denoted by the Greek letter ___.
All particles of an object rotating around the axis of rotation move in a ___ path.
Particles move in a circle lying on a plane that is ___ to the axis.
The relation between arc length $s$ and angular displacement $\theta$ is $s = ___$.
The instantaneous ___ velocity $\omega$ of a rotational object is the same for all particles comprising the object.
The relation between the magnitude of linear velocity $v$ and angular velocity $\omega$ is $v = ___$.
The direction of linear velocity is ___ to the path.
The tangential acceleration $a_T$ relates to angular acceleration $\alpha$ by the equation $a_T = ___$.
Tangential acceleration is a measure of how fast the ___ changes.
The direction of the angular velocity vector is along the ___ of rotation.
According to the ___-hand rule, the thumb points in the direction of the angular velocity vector.
When the angular velocity is ___, the angular acceleration vector points in the same direction as the angular velocity.
When the angular velocity is ___, the angular acceleration vector points in the opposite direction to the angular velocity.
An object that moves in a circle at constant speed $v$ is said to experience ___ circular motion.
In uniform circular motion, the ___ of the velocity remains constant.
The change in ___ of velocity constitutes an acceleration in circular motion.
Acceleration $\vec{a}$ in uniform circular motion points toward the ___ of the circle.
Centripetal acceleration is also called ___ acceleration because it is directed along the radius.
The centripetal acceleration is denoted by the symbol ___.
The equation for centripetal acceleration in terms of linear speed is $a_c = ___$.
Substituting $v = r \omega$ into the centripetal acceleration equation gives $a_c = ___$.
In uniform circular motion, the centripetal acceleration and linear velocity are ___ to each other.
In ___ circular motion, an object moves in a circular path with a varying speed.
Nonuniform circular motion gives rise to ___ acceleration in addition to centripetal acceleration.
The resultant acceleration in nonuniform circular motion is the ___ sum of centripetal and tangential accelerations.
The magnitude of resultant acceleration is $a = \sqrt{a_c^2 + ___}$.
The direction of resultant acceleration is given by $\tan \phi = ___$.
Unlike tangential acceleration, ___ acceleration is present in both uniform and nonuniform circular motion.
